methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $41M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Spiritus is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Charles Cadieu and Matthew Lee that has raised $41M to date. The broader climate and energy sector includes nine private peers (including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen) and one public comparable (LNZA), though direct same-stage and valuation-tier peers within the sector remain sparse.

The stage-progression method is applicable and forms the basis of valuation. Using total capital raised of $41M as a proxy for post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $82M. The peer-median method is not applicable; while nine climate and energy peers exist, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints, making a narrow peer multiple unreliable. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to one applicable method, supports a valuation range of $70M (low) to $94M (high), with a median point of $82M. This range reflects a 15% variance around the stage-progression result. Key uncertainties include the absence of narrow-universe peer validation and reliance on the assumed 2.0x multiplier as representative of market conditions for Series A climate tech. Further valuation refinement would benefit from additional same-stage or recently-closed comparable transactions in the sector.
